'''旧暦5月'''は、[[旧暦]]（[[太陰太陽暦]]）の年初から5番目の[[月 (暦)|月]]である。
[[夏至]]を含む月が5月となる。[[新暦]]では[[5月]]下旬から[[7月]]上旬ごろに当たる。
5月の別名は'''皐月'''（'''さつき'''）である。名前の由来は[[5月]]を参照のこと。
5月30日は存在しない年もある
== 関連項目 == {{月 (暦)|旧暦5月|旧暦360日|月 (旧暦)|5|日 (旧暦)|[[5月30日 (旧暦)|30]]}}

'''旧暦5月'''（きゅうれきごがつ）は、[[旧暦]]（[[太陰太陽暦]]）の年初から5番目の[[月 (暦)|月]]である。
[[夏至]]を含む月が5月となる。[[新暦]]では[[5月]]下旬から[[7月]]上旬ごろに当たる。
5月の別名は'''皐月'''（'''さつき'''）である。名前の由来は[[5月]]を参照のこと。
5月30日は存在しない年もある。
== 関連項目 == {{月 (暦)|旧暦5月|旧暦360日|月 (旧暦)|5|日 (旧暦)|[[5月30日 (旧暦)|30]]}}

'''旧暦5月'''（きゅうれきごがつ）は、[[旧暦]]（[[太陰太陽暦]]）の年初から5番目の[[月 (暦)|月]]である。
[[夏至]]を含む月が5月となる。[[新暦]]では[[5月]]下旬から[[7月]]上旬ごろに当たる。
5月の別名は'''皐月'''（'''さつき'''）である。名前の由来は[[5月]]を参照のこと。
5月30日は存在しない年もある。
== 関連項目 == {{月 (暦)|旧暦5月|旧暦360日|月 (旧暦)|5|日 (旧暦)|[[5月30日 (旧暦)|30]]}}
[[ko:음력 5월]]

'''旧暦5月'''（きゅうれきごがつ）は、[[和暦]]（[[太陰太陽暦]]の[[旧暦]]）の年初から5番目の[[月 (暦)|月]]である。
[[夏至]]を含む月が5月となる。[[グレゴリオ暦]]（[[新暦]]）では[[5月]]下旬から[[7月]]上旬ごろに当たる。
5月の別名は'''皐月'''（'''さつき'''）である。名前の由来は[[5月]]を参照のこと。
東洋の太陰太陽暦では月の日数である[[大小]]（大月30日、小月29日）が年により異なるため、5月29日までで5月30日は存在しない年もある。
== 関連項目 == *[[梅雨]] {{月 (暦)|旧暦5月|旧暦360日|月 (旧暦)|5|日 (旧暦)|[[5月30日 (旧暦)|30]]}}
[[ko:음력 5월]]

[[夏至]]を含む月が5月となる。[[グレゴリオ暦]]（[[新暦]]）では[[5月]]下旬から[[7月]]上旬ごろに当たる。
5月の別名は'''皐月'''（さつき）である。名前の由来は[[5月]]を参照のこと。

revision.text:
{{Lunisolar May}} '''Lunisolar May''' (旧暦五月) is the fifth {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月|12=draft}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=draft}} (the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Summer Solstice|ja|夏至|12=draft}} becomes May. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar|12=draft}}), it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|May|ja|5月|12=draft}} to early {{ill|July|ja|7月|12=draft}}.
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|May|ja|5月|12=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|12=draft}} {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=draft}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
== Satsuki Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Satsuki Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! {{Lunisolar May}} '''Lunisolar May''' (旧暦五月) is the fifth {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月|12=draft}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=draft}} (the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Summer Solstice|ja|夏至|12=draft}} becomes May. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar|12=draft}}), it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|May|ja|5月|12=draft}} to early {{ill|July|ja|7月|12=draft}}.
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|May|ja|5月|12=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|12=draft}} {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=draft}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
== Satsuki Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Satsuki Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! revision.text:
{{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''Lunisolar May''' (旧暦五月) is the fifth [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|qq=}} (the [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]’s {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}).
The month that includes the [[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]] becomes May. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|qq=}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=}}. The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. Another theory holds that the element “sa” itself carries the meaning of rice planting, making “Satsuki” simply “month of rice planting.” Additionally, the character 皐 originally denotes rice ears offered to the gods.
In works such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the characters “五月” were written and read as “Satsuki,” and it was only later that the name began to be written as 皐月. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. According to the [[:en:Erya|Erya]], in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|May}} is said to derive from the goddess Maia ({{lang|la|Maia}}) of fertility in [[:en:Roman mythology|Roman mythology]]. Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.
== Satsuki Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Satsuki Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! Number of Days |- | 2014 || [[:en:May 29|May 29]] || [[:en:June 26|June 26]] || 29 days |- | 2015 || [[:en:June 16|June 16]] || [[:en:July 15|July 15]] || 30 days |- | 2016 || [[:en:June 5|June 5]] || [[:en:July 3|July 3]] || 29 days |- | 2017 || [[:en:May 26|May 26]] || [[:en:June 23|June 23]] || 29 days |- | 2017 ([[:en:Leap month|leap]]) || [[:en:June 24|June 24]] || [[:en:July 22|July 22]] || 29 days |- | 2018 || [[:en:June 14|June 14]] || [[:en:July 12|July 12]] || 29 days |- | 2019 || [[:en:June 3|June 3]] || [[:en:July 2|July 2]] || 30 days |- | 2020 || [[:en:June 21|June 21]] || [[:en:July 20|July 20]] || 30 days |- | 2021 || [[:en:June 10|June 10]] || [[:en:July 9|July 9]] || 30 days |- | 2022 || [[:en:May 30|May 30]] || [[:en:June 28|June 28]] || 30 days |- | 2023 || [[:en:June 18|June 18]] || [[:en:July 17|July 17]] || 30 days |- | 2024 || [[:en:June 6|June 6]] || [[:en:July 5|July 5]] || 30 days |- | 2025 || [[:en:May 27|May 27]] || [[:en:June 24|June 24]] || 29 days |}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q839049]] Wikidata name: 5th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''Lunisolar May''' (旧暦五月) is the fifth [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|qq=}} (the [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]’s {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}).
The month that includes the [[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]] becomes May. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|qq=}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=}}. The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. Another theory holds that the element “sa” itself carries the meaning of rice planting, making “Satsuki” simply “month of rice planting.” Additionally, the character 皐 originally denotes rice ears offered to the gods.
In works such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the characters “五月” were written and read as “Satsuki,” and it was only later that the name began to be written as 皐月. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. According to the [[:en:Erya|Erya]], in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|May}} is said to derive from the goddess Maia ({{lang|la|Maia}}) of fertility in [[:en:Roman mythology|Roman mythology]]. Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.

In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|qq=|1=Eastern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125474644}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q839049]] Wikidata name: 5th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''Lunisolar May''' (旧暦五月) is the fifth [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|qq=|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} (the [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]’s {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}).
The month that includes the [[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]] becomes May. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|qq=|1=Eastern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125474644}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. Another theory holds that the element “sa” itself carries the meaning of rice planting, making “Satsuki” simply “month of rice planting.” Additionally, the character 皐 originally denotes rice ears offered to the gods.
In works such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the characters “五月” were written and read as “Satsuki,” and it was only later that the name began to be written as 皐月. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. According to the [[:en:Erya|Erya]], in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史|1=history of China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82972}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|May}} is said to derive from the goddess Maia ({{lang|la|Maia}}) of fertility in [[:en:Roman mythology|Roman mythology]]. Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.

[[ar:May (Lunisolar)]] <references group="lower-alpha"/> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]] [[za:Ndwenngux]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:五月]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]] [[za:Ndwenngux]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:五月]] {{wikidata link|Q839049}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]][[ja:5月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월]][[th:เดือน 5]][[za:Ndwenngux]][[zh min nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]][[zh_yue:五月]][[zh:农历五月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q839049]] Wikidata name: 5th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''Lunisolar May''' (旧暦五月) is the fifth [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|qq=|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} (the [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]’s {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}).
The month that includes the [[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]] becomes May. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|qq=|1=Eastern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125474644}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. Another theory holds that the element “sa” itself carries the meaning of rice planting, making “Satsuki” simply “month of rice planting.” Additionally, the character 皐 originally denotes rice ears offered to the gods.
In works such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the characters “五月” were written and read as “Satsuki,” and it was only later that the name began to be written as 皐月. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. According to the [[:en:Erya|Erya]], in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史|1=history of China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82972}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|May}} is said to derive from the goddess Maia ({{lang|la|Maia}}) of fertility in [[:en:Roman mythology|Roman mythology]]. Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.

The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. Another theory holds that the element “sa” itself carries the meaning of rice planting, making “Satsuki” simply “month of rice planting.” Additionally, the character 皐 originally denotes rice ears offered to the gods.
In works such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the characters “五月” were written and read as “Satsuki,” and it was only later that the name began to be written as 皐月. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. According to the [[:en:Erya|Erya]], in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史|1=history of China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82972}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|May}} is said to derive from the goddess Maia ({{lang|la|Maia}}) of fertility in [[:en:Roman mythology|Roman mythology]]. Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]][[ja:5月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월]][[th:เดือน 5]][[za:Ndwenngux]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:五月]][[zh:农历五月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839049]] Wikidata name: 5th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''Lunisolar May''' (旧暦五月) is the fifth [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|qq=|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} (the [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]’s {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}).
The month that includes the [[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]] becomes May. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|qq=|1=Eastern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125474644}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. Another theory holds that the element “sa” itself carries the meaning of rice planting, making “Satsuki” simply “month of rice planting.” Additionally, the character 皐 originally denotes rice ears offered to the gods.
In works such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the characters “五月” were written and read as “Satsuki,” and it was only later that the name began to be written as 皐月. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. According to the [[:en:Erya|Erya]], in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史|1=history of China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82972}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]][[ja:5月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월]][[th:เดือน 5]][[za:Ndwenngux]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:五月]][[zh:农历五月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839049]] Wikidata name: 5th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''Lunisolar May''' (旧暦五月) is the fifth [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} (the [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]’s {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}).
The month that includes the [[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]] becomes May. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|1=Eastern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125474644}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. Another theory holds that the element “sa” itself carries the meaning of rice planting, making “Satsuki” simply “month of rice planting.” Additionally, the character 皐 originally denotes rice ears offered to the gods.
In works such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the characters “五月” were written and read as “Satsuki,” and it was only later that the name began to be written as 皐月. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. According to the [[:en:Erya|Erya]], in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史|1=history of China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82972}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|May}} is said to derive from the goddess Maia ({{lang|la|Maia}}) of fertility in [[:en:Roman mythology|Roman mythology]]. Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]][[ja:5月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월]][[th:เดือน 5]][[za:Ndwenngux]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:五月]][[zh:农历五月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839049]] Wikidata name: 5th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''Lunisolar May''' (旧暦五月) is the fifth [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} (the [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]’s {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}).
The month that includes the [[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]] becomes May. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|1=Eastern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125474644}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. Another theory holds that the element “sa” itself carries the meaning of rice planting, making “Satsuki” simply “month of rice planting.” Additionally, the character 皐 originally denotes rice ears offered to the gods.
In works such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the characters “五月” were written and read as “Satsuki,” and it was only later that the name began to be written as 皐月. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. According to the [[:en:Erya|Erya]], in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史|1=history of China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82972}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|May}} is said to derive from the goddess Maia ({{lang|la|Maia}}) of fertility in [[:en:Roman mythology|Roman mythology]]. Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.
== Satsuki Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Satsuki Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! Number of Days |- | 2014 || [[:en:May 29|May 29]] || [[:en:June 26|June 26]] || 29 days |- | 2015 || [[:en:June 16|June 16]] || [[:en:July 15|July 15]] || 30 days |- | 2016 || [[:en:June 5|June 5]] || [[:en:July 3|July 3]] || 29 days |- | 2017 || [[:en:May 26|May 26]] || [[:en:June 23|June 23]] || 29 days |- | 2017 ([[:en:Leap month|leap]]) || [[:en:June 24|June 24]] || [[:en:July 22|July 22]] || 29 days |- | 2018 || [[:en:June 14|June 14]] || [[:en:July 12|July 12]] || 29 days |- | 2019 || [[:en:June 3|June 3]] || [[:en:July 2|July 2]] || 30 days |- | 2020 || [[:en:June 21|June 21]] || [[:en:July 20|July 20]] || 30 days |- | 2021 || [[:en:June 10|June 10]] || [[:en:July 9|July 9]] || 30 days |- | 2022 || [[:en:May 30|May 30]] || [[:en:June 28|June 28]] || 30 days |- | 2023 || [[:en:June 18|June 18]] || [[:en:July 17|July 17]] || 30 days |- | 2024 || [[:en:June 6|June 6]] || [[:en:July 5|July 5]] || 30 days |- | 2025 || [[:en:May 27|May 27]] || [[:en:June 24|June 24]] || 29 days |}

[[ar:May (Lunisolar)]] <references group="lower-alpha"/> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]] [[za:Ndwenngux]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:五月]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]] [[za:Ndwenngux]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:五月]] {{wikidata link|Q839049}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]][[ja:5月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월]][[th:เดือน 5]][[za:Ndwenngux]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:五月]][[zh:农历五月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839049]] Wikidata name: 5th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''Lunisolar May''' (旧暦五月) is the fifth [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} (the [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]’s {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}).
The month that includes the [[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]] becomes May. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|1=Eastern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125474644}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. Another theory holds that the element “sa” itself carries the meaning of rice planting, making “Satsuki” simply “month of rice planting.” Additionally, the character 皐 originally denotes rice ears offered to the gods.
In works such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the characters “五月” were written and read as “Satsuki,” and it was only later that the name began to be written as 皐月. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. According to the [[:en:Erya|Erya]], in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史|1=history of China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82972}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|May}} is said to derive from the goddess Maia ({{lang|la|Maia}}) of fertility in [[:en:Roman mythology|Roman mythology]]. Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.

According to the [[:en:Erya|Erya]], in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史|1=history of China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82972}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|May}} is said to derive from the goddess Maia ({{lang|la|Maia}}) of fertility in [[:en:Roman mythology|Roman mythology]]. Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]][[ja:5月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월]][[th:เดือน 5]][[za:Ndwenngux]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:五月]][[zh:农历五月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839049]] Wikidata name: 5th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''Lunisolar May''' (旧暦五月) is the fifth [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} (the [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]’s {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}).
The month that includes the [[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]] becomes May. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|1=Eastern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125474644}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. Another theory holds that the element “sa” itself carries the meaning of rice planting, making “Satsuki” simply “month of rice planting.” Additionally, the character 皐 originally denotes rice ears offered to the gods.
In works such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the characters “五月” were written and read as “Satsuki,” and it was only later that the name began to be written as 皐月. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. According to the [[:en:Erya|Erya]], in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史|1=history of China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82972}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|May}} is said to derive from the goddess Maia ({{lang|la|Maia}}) of fertility in [[:en:Roman mythology|Roman mythology]]. Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.

The month that includes the [[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]] becomes May. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|1=Eastern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125474644}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. Another theory holds that the element “sa” itself carries the meaning of rice planting, making “Satsuki” simply “month of rice planting.” Additionally, the character 皐 originally denotes rice ears offered to the gods.
In works such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the characters “五月” were written and read as “Satsuki,” and it was only later that the name began to be written as 皐月. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. According to the [[:en:Erya|Erya]], in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史|1=history of China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82972}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|May}} is said to derive from the goddess Maia ({{lang|la|Maia}}) of fertility in [[:en:Roman mythology|Roman mythology]]. Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]][[ja:5月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월]][[th:เดือน 5]][[za:Ndwenngux]][[zh_min_nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]][[zh_yue:五月]][[zh:农历五月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]][[ja:5月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월]][[th:เดือน 5]][[za:Ndwenngux]][[zh_min_nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]][[zh_yue:五月]][[zh:农历五月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]][[ja:5月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월]][[th:เดือน 5]][[za:Ndwenngux]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:五月]][[zh:农历五月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839049]] Wikidata name: 5th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''Lunisolar May''' (旧暦五月) is the fifth [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} (the [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]’s {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}).
The month that includes the [[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]] becomes May. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|1=Eastern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125474644}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. Another theory holds that the element “sa” itself carries the meaning of rice planting, making “Satsuki” simply “month of rice planting.” Additionally, the character 皐 originally denotes rice ears offered to the gods.
In works such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the characters “五月” were written and read as “Satsuki,” and it was only later that the name began to be written as 皐月. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. According to the [[:en:Erya|Erya]], in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史|1=history of China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82972}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|May}} is said to derive from the goddess Maia ({{lang|la|Maia}}) of fertility in [[:en:Roman mythology|Roman mythology]]. Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.

The month that includes the [[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]] becomes May. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].

In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|1=Eastern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125474644}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.

The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|1=Eastern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125474644}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. Another theory holds that the element “sa” itself carries the meaning of rice planting, making “Satsuki” simply “month of rice planting.” Additionally, the character 皐 originally denotes rice ears offered to the gods.
In works such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], the characters “五月” were written and read as “Satsuki,” and it was only later that the name began to be written as 皐月. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. According to the [[:en:Erya|Erya]], in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史|1=history of China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82972}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}} and marks the season of the [[:en:East Asian rainy season|plum rain]]. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|May}} is said to derive from the goddess Maia ({{lang|la|Maia}}) of fertility in [[:en:Roman mythology|Roman mythology]]. Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.

The month that includes the [[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]] becomes May. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}), it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:May|May]] to early [[:en:July|July]].
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|1=Eastern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125474644}} [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language}}, May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language}}: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]][[ja:5月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월]][[th:เดือน 5]][[za:Ndwenngux]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:五月]][[zh:农历五月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]][[ja:5月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월]][[th:เดือน 5]][[za:Ndwenngux]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:五月]][[zh:农历五月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 5 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 5-ngie̍t]][[ja:5月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월]][[th:เดือน 5]][[za:Ndwenngux]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 5 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:五月]][[zh:农历五月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839049]] Wikidata name: 5th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''Lunisolar May''' (旧暦五月) is the fifth {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} (the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}’s {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}).
The month that includes the {{ill|Summer solstice|en|Summer solstice|WD=Q7603898|lt=Summer Solstice}} becomes May. In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} ({{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}), it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|May|en|May|WD=Q119|lt=May}} to early {{ill|July|en|July|WD=Q121|lt=July}}.
Another name for May is {{nihongo|'''Satsuki'''|さつき|qq=}}. The origin of the name is explained in [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|May|ja|5月|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Eastern|ja|東洋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern|1=Eastern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125474644}} {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies each year, there are years in which May ends on the 29th and May 30th does not exist.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:5月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:31, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, the {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar May|lt_ja=旧暦5月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is called '''Satsuki''' (さつき), and today it is also used as an alternate name for May of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}. The term “Satsuki” derives from “Sanahetsuki” (早苗月; さなへつき), meaning “month of early rice seedlings,” which was later shortened. Another theory holds that the element “sa” itself carries the meaning of rice planting, making “Satsuki” simply “month of rice planting.” Additionally, the character 皐 originally denotes rice ears offered to the gods.
In works such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, the characters “五月” were written and read as “Satsuki,” and it was only later that the name began to be written as 皐月. The notation “皐月” originally appears in Chinese classics as an alternate name for the fifth month of the lunisolar calendar. According to the {{ill|Erya|en|Erya|WD=Q1207685|lt=Erya}}, in {{ill|ancient China|ja|古代中国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient China|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国の歴史|1=history of China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q82972}} the twelve months were each called “陬如寎余皋且相壯玄陽辜涂” (皋 is the original character for 皐).
“Satsuki” is also the name of a flower. Another alternate name is “Ayamezuki” (菖蒲月; あやめづき).
The lunisolar May corresponds to June through July of the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|新暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|1=new calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}} and marks the season of the {{ill|East Asian rainy season|en|East Asian rainy season|WD=Q658486|lt=plum rain}}. “Samidare” (五月雨) is another name for the plum rain, and “Satsukibare” (五月晴れ) originally refers to the clear intervals between showers.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language}}, May is called “Muntukkachup” (ムントゥッカチュㇷ゚; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language}}: mun tukka cup), meaning “month of grass growth”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|May}} is said to derive from the goddess Maia ({{lang|la|Maia}}) of fertility in {{ill|Roman mythology|en|Roman mythology|WD=Q122173|lt=Roman mythology}}. Of the twelve months, it is the only one without an English abbreviation<ref group="note">English month abbreviations are three letters long; May already has three letters.</ref>.
